sources.label: Acuvi AB appoints Jens Lagergren as CFO (Cision, mid-May 2026, effective Jun 1)
Acuvi precision technology enters Formula 1 (Inderes release, pre-Jun 6)
Acuvi receives 1.3 MUSD order from US defense customer (MarketScreener, pre-Jun 6)

verdicts.rankRationale: No material news since June 6; yesterday's verdict already executed the de-rating from #1 to #4 on scale/indirectness grounds, so re-moving without new information would be churn. Today's -2.7% drift on a -27% YTD tape with the precision-piezo thesis intact (recent defense and F1 orders still standing) marginally improves asymmetry, justifying a small positive drift at the current rank.
verdicts.bull: Sub-$50M cap piezo precision-motion specialist with fresh US defense ($1.3M) and F1 development orders proving the technology; if precision actuation content spreads into humanoid hands, grippers, and compact modules, the rerating runway from these levels is large.
verdicts.neutral: Orders to date are small and development-stage; the robotics-hands optionality remains unproven, so the stock trades on micro-cap flow until a robotics-specific design win or revenue inflection lands.
verdicts.bear: A $40M-cap, thinly traded micro-cap down 27% YTD can keep bleeding regardless of thesis; dilution risk and dependence on a handful of customers mean the robotics option could expire worthless before commercialization arrives.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: A named design win or volume order tying Acuvi's piezo actuation into robotics/humanoid end-products, or H2-2026 delivery confirmations on the defense and F1 orders.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Micro-cap financing/dilution risk and continued order droughts, with liquidity so thin that flow alone can drive further drawdowns.
